bugs.title: Stream ends with 'no response from AI' error after second create_itinerary_items batch
bugs.description: During selection mode, the AI called create_itinerary_items twice in the same turn (7 items first, then 4 items). After the second batch, the stream emitted an error: {"error":{"message":"no response from AI — please try again"}}. The itinerary items were created successfully, and the text response was mostly delivered before the error. This appears to be a tool loop iteration issue where the AI exhausted its tool loop budget.
bugs.expected: Stream should complete cleanly with a messageComplete event
bugs.actual: Stream ended with error after successful tool execution

bugs.title: Planning session mode mismatch — session created in selection mode shows as CHAT_MODE_PLANNING
bugs.description: The session b0b60740 was created during a CHAT_MODE_SELECTION SendMessage call, but ListChatSessions reports it as CHAT_MODE_PLANNING. The first message (trip creation) was sent in selection mode but the session was later reused for planning messages, which may explain the mode being overwritten. However, the initial selection-mode message is not visible in GetChatHistory for this session — only the 3 planning conversations appear (6 messages). The selection-mode conversation may have been lost or stored differently.
bugs.expected: Selection mode message should be retrievable in chat history, or a separate session should exist for it
bugs.actual: Selection mode message content not in GetChatHistory; session shows as planning mode only

ux_issues.description: The AI response in selection mode was very long — it generated a full itinerary overview, booking recommendations, and 11 itinerary items all in the first message. This could overwhelm a user who just described their trip. A more staged approach (create trip, ask clarifying questions, then build itinerary) would be better UX.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider having the AI ask clarifying questions (dates, budget specifics, dietary restrictions) before generating a full itinerary in the first response.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: AI did not ask clarifying questions before building itinerary
ai_behavior_issues.context: The persona test specifies the AI should ask about kids' interests, swimming abilities, and dietary restrictions before finalizing recommendations. Instead, the AI jumped straight to building a 10-day itinerary with 11 items on the very first message, without asking any clarifying questions.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Second text response in selection mode started with 'I am so sorry about that — it looks like my previous response didn't make it through!'
ai_behavior_issues.context: After the second create_itinerary_items call, the AI generated a second full response that started with an apology suggesting the first response was lost. This is confusing to the user who already received the first response via streaming text deltas. The AI appears to have lost context of its own previous output within the same turn.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The AI performed very well as a family travel companion. It created a well-structured 10-day Costa Rica itinerary with realistic driving times, proactively triggered an expert handoff to a Costa Rica nature/family specialist, and gave excellent safety advice specific to Noah (age 6, non-swimmer) and Lily's medical concerns. Companion mode responses were appropriately concise and practical, especially the emergency bee sting scenario which gave immediate clinic info. The main gap was not asking clarifying questions before generating the full itinerary — the AI assumed rather than asked. Context injection worked flawlessly: the AI never asked where the family was going after trip creation, and consistently referenced Costa Rica, the kids' names and ages, and specific family details throughout all conversations.
